content:
Hi; I'm following the instructions at https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/Documentation/rust/quick-start.rst but after running cargo install --locked --version $(scripts/min-tool-version.sh bindgen) bindgen there is still no bindgen command available, which results in
```$ make LLVM=1 rustavailable

*** Rust bindings generator 'bindgen' could not be found.```
hm, if I do make LLVM=1 BINDGEN=/home/ubuntu/.cargo/bin/bindgen rustavailable then it works.
Am I right to assume that you installed rust from your distro's package manager rather than rustup and as such never had to add ~/.cargo/bin to your PATH to be able to run rustc?
yes, correct, I used sudo snap install --classic rustup
(that was the suggestion from my OS after running rustup and not finding it)

Right, using a globally installed rustup will put the rustc and cargo wrappers in a global location rather than ~/.cargo/bin and thus ~/.cargo/bin doesn't need to be added to PATH unless you are using cargo install. Maybe make rustavailable could warn if ~/.cargo/bin/bindgen exists, but ~/.cargo/bin is not in PATH?
